stories.title: Trump Touts Iran Progress Amid Hormuz Strike Reports
stories.summary: US and Israeli jets struck a number of Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, local media reported, hours after President Donald Trump had suggested negotiations with Tehran over an interim deal were progressing. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski breaks down what we know about the state of the talks. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Gold Holds Gain as Iran Deal Progress Eases Inflation Concerns
stories.summary: Gold held gains on signs the US and Iran were making progress on reaching a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore oil flows, easing inflation fears.

stories.title: US Launches New Strikes on Iran Even as Trump Touts Progress for Deal
stories.summary: US and Israeli jets struck a number of Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, hours after President Donald Trump had suggested negotiations with Tehran over an interim deal were progressing.

stories.title: Japan Cablemaker Rout Exposes Cracks in AI Infrastructure Rally
stories.summary: A $40 billion selloff in a 141-year-old Japanese cable firm has served as a reality check on the fragility of the global AI-driven stock rally.

stories.title: Treasury Curve Flashes Higher-for-Longer Warning Under Warsh
stories.summary: A key Treasury yield gap has shrunk to its tightest level in a year as traders ramp up bets the Federal Reserve may keep interest rates higher for longer under new chairman Kevin Warsh.

stories.title: Oil Advances as Strikes Near Strait Cloud Outlook for Iran Deal
stories.summary: Oil advanced after slumping by more than 7% on Monday, as reports of fresh military strikes in Iran clouded the outlook for an interim deal between Tehran and Washington to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: CNBC Daily Open: Faith in markets, doubts in diplomacy
stories.summary: Markets remain optimistic as talks inch toward an Iran deal.

stories.title: India’s Scorching Nights Strain Power Grid and Threaten Health
stories.summary: India’s dangerously torrid summer days have long been a climate warning. Now the country is increasingly dealing with sweltering nights too, as global warming and poor urban planning put millions at risk.

stories.title: US strikes Iranian missile sites as peace talks continue
stories.summary: Washington announces ‘self-defence’ operation against targets in southern Iran

stories.title: China’s change in maths on carbon emissions masks growth, report says
stories.summary: Independent analysis suggests retrospective change to data indicating climate change progress

stories.title: Korea Short-Term Debt Pricing Too Many BOK Hikes, NH Amundi Says
stories.summary: South Korea’s bond market is pricing in an excessive number of central bank rate hikes, creating buying opportunities in short-term debt, according to NH Amundi Asset Management Co.

stories.title: Israel to Intensify Lebanon Strikes Amid US-Iran Deal Talks
stories.summary: Israel will intensify its strikes against Hezbollah, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Monday, amid stepped-up negotiations between the US and Iran to bring an end to the conflict in the region.

stories.title: US chipmakers in China see revenue rise, defying trade tensions: Hurun list
stories.summary: Twenty-six US semiconductor firms, from Qualcomm to Nvidia, saw their China revenue climb by an average of 20 per cent last year, despite trade tensions, according to a new analysis. The data, featured in the latest “Hurun Top 100 US Enterprises in China 2026” report released by the Hurun Research Institute on Monday, tracks the performance of 100 publicly listed American companies based on their

stories.title: U.S. conducts 'self-defense strikes' in Iran as Trump tries to push for peace deal
stories.summary: Targets included missile launch sites and Iranian boats attempting to emplace mines.

stories.title: A new battlefront for KFC and its Chinese rivals: the lighter meal
stories.summary: Fast-food restaurants in mainland China have found a magic bullet for both health and company profit: offering consumers meal options that limit sugar and salt and provide balanced nutrition. Lighter meals are rising in popularity in China, with an estimated consumer base of about 33 million across the country, and are becoming a game-changer in the food and drink sector, with big-name restaurant

stories.title: Iran Signals Potential Easing of Internet Blackout Amid US Talks
stories.summary: Iran’s government could soon roll back some restrictions on a months-long digital blackout that has cut off millions of its citizens from the internet, the official Islamic Republic News Agency said, as diplomatic talks between the US and Tehran gather pace.

stories.summary: Donald Trump also said negotiations with Iran were "proceeding nicely," but cautioned that the U.S. could resume military action if discussions were to collapse.

stories.title: ASX Ramps Up Capital Spending to Support Technology Upgrade
stories.summary: ASX Ltd. is increasing its capital spending to upgrade critical market infrastructure, as Australia’s embattled exchange operator seeks to regain the confidence of regulators and stakeholders after years of technical issues.

stories.title: South Korea's Kospi hits fresh record as hopes of U.S.-Iran breakthrough rise
stories.summary: Asia-Pacific markets were set to open broadly higher Tuesday, amid hopes for a breakthrough in the U.S.-Iran peace negotiations.

stories.title: Taiwan Overtakes India as World's Fifth-Largest Stock Market
stories.summary: Taiwan overtook India in stock market value, powered mainly by a breakneck rally in the world’s largest chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.

stories.title: India to Host Quad Foreign Ministers' Meeting
stories.summary: Shruti Pandalai, the India Chair of the Lowy Institute, talks to Bloomberg TV about the upcoming Quad meeting in India, where the security situation in the Strait of Hormuz is expected to be high on the agenda. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Treasuries Gain in Catch-Up Trade as Trump Sees Progress on Iran
stories.summary: Treasuries rallied across the curve as cash trading resumed after a holiday, with investors growing more optimistic about a potential US-Iran deal.

stories.title: Xiaomi Short Bets Hit Record on Cost Worries Ahead of Earnings
stories.summary: Traders pushed bearish bets on Xiaomi Corp. to a record ahead of first-quarter earnings amid concerns over rising memory costs and escalating rivalry in China’s electric vehicle market.

stories.title: HSBC Hong Kong targets 7.5 million customers, same number as city’s population
stories.summary: HSBC is targeting an increase in its Hong Kong client base to match the city’s population of 7.5 million, a goal to be driven mainly by international investors, according to its Hong Kong CEO Maggie Ng. stories.title: These AI Gurus Are Charging Wall Street Banks $25,000 a Day
stories.summary: Global banks are pouring billions into artificial intelligence yet struggling to automate workflows. Two ex-bankers say they are being hired to ignite the shift.

stories.title: A Scorching Asian Summer Will Add to Risk of Surging Gas Prices
stories.summary: As the global gas market grapples with the Strait of Hormuz being all-but closed for nearly three months, traders are fixated on two wildcards: China and the weather.

stories.title: Mr. Bing Chili Crisp Aims to Reach Restaurants, Retail
stories.summary: Brian Goldberg, founder and chief growth officer of Mr. Bing foods, said that his brand of chili crisp and new line of Asian barbecue sauces has found success by selling products to both traditional retail and restaurant customers. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Codelco Union Threatens Protests as Misreporting Fallout Spreads
stories.summary: One of Codelco’s biggest unions vowed to fight any attempt to claw back bonuses as fallout from the overstatement of copper output at the Chilean state miner continues to spread.

stories.summary: Stocks trimmed their advance and crude oil climbed after reports of US strikes on sites in Iran damped optimism over a potential deal with Tehran.

stories.summary: Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov called US State Secretary Marco Rubio to advise him to evacuate US citizens and diplomats from Kyiv, as the Kremlin plans to continue heavy strikes on the Ukrainian capital, according to a statement published by the Russian Foreign Ministry Monday.

stories.title: Mexico to Host Iran Players for World Cup as War Tensions Simmer
stories.summary: Mexico will serve as the Iranian national team’s World Cup base after US authorities refused to let the players stay overnight on its soil amid ongoing military tensions.

stories.title: Nigeria’s First Quarter GDP Eases on Slowdown in Oil Sector
stories.summary: Nigeria’s economy grew at a slower-than-expected pace in the first quarter as growth in both its oil and non-oil sectors eased.

stories.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping thanked visiting Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif for Pakistan’s work in mediating talks between the US and Iran — underscoring Beijing’s push to end a war that poses a range of risks to the world’s second-biggest economy.

stories.title: Move over, seltzer. Non-carbonated drinks are taking the spotlight
stories.summary: Non-carbonated alcoholic drinks like Surfside and BeatBox are stealing "share of throat" from hard seltzers, particularly among Gen Z.

stories.summary: A spike in energy costs hasn’t yet turned into second-round effects in other parts of the euro-area economy, according to outgoing European Central Bank Governing Council member Francois Villeroy de Galhau.

stories.summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Alberta’s voters should be wary of separatists promoting a “dangerous bluff,” citing his own experience in leading the Bank of England during the Brexit referendum.

stories.title: Mexico Exports Rise to Their Highest Level on Record in April
stories.summary: Mexico’s trade surplus widened far more than expected in April to $4.52 billion, well above the median estimate of $476.35 million in a Bloomberg survey, as exports climbed to the highest since the data began in 1980. The surge highlights how the country continues to strengthen its role as a manufacturing hub for the US economy as Washington takes a tougher stance on trade. The cumulative trade su

stories.title: Czech Premier Urges Interest Rate Cut as Inflation Risks Grow
stories.summary: The Czech Republic’s prime minister urged the central bank to cut interest rates, doubling down on his push for lower borrowing costs even as the country faces growing inflation risks from energy prices.

stories.title: BHP Cuts Green Push in Iron Ore Segment, Media Report Says
stories.summary: BHP Group is pulling back on key decarbonization projects in its Western Australian iron ore operations, slowing a climate strategy the miner had once positioned as central to its long-term growth plans, according to leaked internal documents cited by the Guardian and ABC’s Four Corners.

stories.title: As Chinese EV makers expand in Europe, are local automotive suppliers set to benefit?

stories.title: Oil Sands Firms Can Afford Carbon Capture, Canadian Energy Minister Says
stories.summary: Canadian Energy Minister Tim Hodgson said he’s “highly confident” that Alberta oil sands companies can absorb the cost of building carbon capture, despite their protests that Canada’s climate rules hurt their global competitiveness.

stories.title: Japan Won’t Add Bonds on Calendar Basis to Fund Extra Budget
stories.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said the government will finance its extra budget without increasing bond issuance on a calendar basis, in a likely attempt to ease market concerns over government finances.

stories.title: Alibaba’s Qwen catches up with ‘Sharif speed’ to help forge Pakistan deal
stories.summary: On the first leg of his four-day China visit, Pakistan’s prime minister – famed for “Sharif speed”, a term describing his swift execution of development projects – met his match in a leading AI tool. Shehbaz Sharif, keen on accelerating his nation’s digital economy with the help of Chinese firms, issued a surprise request during his visit to Alibaba’s headquarters in Hangzhou on Sunday afternoon.
